title: How to create Glossary for our team?
excerpt: I have searched and not found any suggestions. I have tried a few ideas using different features but not sold on any of them.  We have unique terminology in our team and need a glossary for internal use so that team is u…

posts.author: Allie12380 (Monica Davis)
posts.cookedHtml: I have searched and not found any suggestions. I have tried a few ideas using different features but not sold on any of them. We have unique terminology in our team and need a glossary for internal use so that team is using vocabulary consistently. Would appreciate any suggestions or recommendations as to how best to create glossary within Monday.com for our team’s unique terminology.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Allie12380,

Firstly, welcome to the Community

I believe there are a couple of ways you could set this up!

  1. Setting up a terminology workdoc that your team can refer back to: monday workdocs

  2. Creating a terminology board - each word makes up an item in the board, and either The Updates Section or perhaps the text column can be used as a space for the terminology description.

Hopefully this helps in some way!

posts.author: prabu (Prabu)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Allie12380 ,

If you’re still looking for a solution that’s a little bit more purpose built and integrated, you can look into an app that I have developed an app called Team Glossary.

The key feature is that it auto-detects your team’s unique terminology right inside the item view. This way, the team gets the definitions they need in context without having to leave their work to search a different board or doc.

Here is the link in case you’d like to try it out: App Link
